Finding: Hybrid keyframe→video is the primary bet for walk cycles
Evidence: Video models' first/last frame conditioning directly solves the temporal pose disambiguation problem that image-only fails at

Finding: Build a model-agnostic adapter layer using Factory/Adapter/Strategy patterns
Evidence: Model landscape shifting too fast (Kling 3.0 launched Feb 5, 2026) to commit to one vendor

Finding: Post-processing (pixel quantization) is the critical engineering challenge
Evidence: Video models output anti-aliased, high-res frames that need aggressive palette snapping, grid alignment, and alpha recovery

Finding: Keep image-only as a fallback/keyframe generator
Evidence: Image-only works for static poses, VFX frames, and generating the anchor keyframes that feed into video interpolation

Finding: Audit/validation pipeline is the durable, compounding asset
Evidence: Quality gates, retry ladders, TexturePacker integration, and Phaser validation are model-agnostic and survive every model transition

Finding: Pika Pikaframes 2.2 (up to 5 keyframes) is a top candidate
Evidence: Multi-keyframe interpolation maps directly to walk cycle anchoring (frames 0, 2, 4, 6 → interpolate 1, 3, 5, 7)
